channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Oil and gas companies making hay by making plastic?
item.description: Author Beth Gardiner talks about her new book, 'Plastic Inc.' and about what she learned about the relationship between oil and gas companies and the plastics industry.
item.content:encoded: Author Beth Gardiner talks about her new book, 'Plastic Inc.' and about what she learned about the relationship between oil and gas companies and the plastics industry.
item.dc:creator: Kai McNamee

item.title: An inevitable rise in Chinese-made EVs in America?
item.description: Chinese-made EVs that are not currently legally sold in the U.S. are making their way across the border anyway, says Wall Street Journal reporter Ryan Felton, who recently covered a rise in interest in the vehicles.
item.content:encoded: Chinese-made EVs that are not currently legally sold in the U.S. are making their way across the border anyway, says Wall Street Journal reporter Ryan Felton, who recently covered a rise in interest in the vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ofman

item.title: Spirit Airlines ceases operations after escalating financial struggles
item.description: The low-cost carrier, which had been struggling for years, announced it will cease operations. Spirit had been seeking a $500 million lifeline from the White House, but talks failed to yield a deal.
item.content:encoded:
The low-cost carrier, which had been struggling for years, announced it will cease operations. Spirit had been seeking a $500 million lifeline from the White House, but talks failed to yield a deal.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Iran war shakes Global economy as energy costs surge and recession fears grow
item.description: From energy markets to everyday prices, the fallout from the Iran war is reshaping the global economy.
item.content:encoded: From energy markets to everyday prices, the fallout from the Iran war is reshaping the global economy.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: How well can EVs handle the heat — and the cold? AAA put them to the test
item.description: Electric vehicles lose some range in the winter — and, to a lesser degree, in the summer. But exactly how much? AAA has brand-new data.
item.content:encoded:
Electric vehicles lose some range in the winter — and, to a lesser degree, in the summer. But exactly how much? AAA has brand-new data.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Trump gives the go-ahead for a major new Canada-U.S. oil pipeline
item.description: More state and federal approvals are needed for the 3-foot-wide Bridger Pipeline Expansion, which would stretch from the Canadian border with Montana down through eastern Montana and Wyoming, where it would link up with another pipeline.
item.content:encoded:
More state and federal approvals are needed for the 3-foot-wide Bridger Pipeline Expansion, which would stretch from the Canadian border with Montana down through eastern Montana and Wyoming, where it would link up with another pipeline.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The economy is growing – but soaring energy prices could put a damper on that
item.description: The U.S. economy grew at an annual pace of 2% in the first three months of the year. That's an improvement from the previous quarter. But soaring energy prices could put a damper on growth.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. economy grew at an annual pace of 2% in the first three months of the year. That's an improvement from the previous quarter. But soaring energy prices could put a damper on growth.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: How Spirit Airlines grew so fast — and why it's experiencing so much turbulence now
item.description: As Spirit Airlines hangs on the brink of liquidation, we look back at how it grew so fast, and how the bigger airlines fought back to beat them at their own game.
item.content:encoded: As Spirit Airlines hangs on the brink of liquidation, we look back at how it grew so fast, and how the bigger airlines fought back to beat them at their own game.
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: The Onion's bid to take over Infowars moves to the Texas Supreme Court
item.description: The state's highest court will now consider a deal that would allow the Onion to license the Infowars brand name and turn the show into a mockery of itself.
item.content:encoded:
The state's highest court will now consider a deal that would allow the Onion to license the Infowars brand name and turn the show into a mockery of itself.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith

item.title: These fans are boycotting the World Cup. Will they make it a bust?
item.description: Some fans in the U.S. and around the world are unhappy with World Cup ticket prices — and U.S. immigration policies. So they're deciding not to come, raising concerns across the travel industry.
item.content:encoded:
Some fans in the U.S. and around the world are unhappy with World Cup ticket prices — and U.S. immigration policies. So they're deciding not to come, raising concerns across the travel industry. 

(Image credit: Ty Malugani)
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam
